plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to lower single name concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad quality exposure.
Add VRT at 5% as a high-quality growth setup.
This plan aims for capital preservation with a large cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and low drawdown. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but potential upside is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leverage and exit positions below stop.
Reduce SMH to 10% to lower exposure to a downtrending sector.
Increase QQQI to 22% for balanced income and quality growth.
Add VRT at 8% as a strong risk/reward growth setup.
This plan aims for risk-first growth with a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, balancing growth with risk management. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed, aiming for a more stable growth trajectory with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reallocate capital.
Reduce SMH to 5% to minimize exposure to a downtrending sector.
Buy GUSH at 7% for a high risk/reward setup and sector diversification.
Add VRT at 10% and AVGO at 8% as strong, high-scoring growth setups.
This plan aggressively leans into strong setups with strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month by concentrating in high-scoring setups and using leverage. Be aware this mode significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown risk. Leveraged ETFs like SOXL/SPAL (if added) decay daily and can lead to substantial losses quickly; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta and downtrending exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income and covered-call exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
A significant cash buffer will be held for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs. This is not a savings account; some market risk remains.
